subject: Update npm.registry
commit_message:
Update npm.registry

Change-Id: Id8ab1294458b9ebc8e16273e3c9cc7476e4e771e
Issue-ID: CIMAN-403
Signed-off-by: Vasyl Razinkov <vasyl.razinkov@est.tech>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

You should still be able to use the npm.public repository.
This is a group repository that also contains the following two repositories.
* npm.release which is a local repository for locally produced items (as far as I understand)
* npm which is a proxy pointing towards https://registry.npmjs.org/, and which is online (I activated it last week).

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/ci-management-jjb-verify/5508/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/ci-management-jjb-verify/5508

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> You should still be able to use the npm.public repository.
> This is a group repository that also contains the following two repositories.
> * npm.release which is a local repository for locally produced items (as far as I understand)
> * npm which is a proxy pointing towards https://registry.npmjs.org/, and which is online (I activated it last week).

yes, we still using npm.public repository, and it still producing failures 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/sdc-honolulu-verify-java/10/
https://jenkins.onap.org/job/sdc-integration-tests-honolulu-verify-java/12/
https://jenkins.onap.org/job/sdc-integration-tests-honolulu-verify-java/6/

so, should we switch to 'new' proxy or stick with old one and you fix issue different way?

Patch Set 1:

we're still using npm.public, but need TWO DIFFERENT URLs.  The first is found in our pom (which I'm guessing is similar to these yaml files) - we had to change it to this:
https://nexus3.onap.org/repository/npm.public/npm/-/

that works in conjunction with the second, which appears in the npmrc:
https://nexus3.onap.org/repository/npm.public/

Note: the first URL is needed to download the npm.tar.gz, while the second is needed to download all of its components.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> we're still using npm.public, but need TWO DIFFERENT URLs.  The first is found in our pom (which I'm guessing is similar to these yaml files) - we had to change it to this:
> https://nexus3.onap.org/repository/npm.public/npm/-/
> 
> that works in conjunction with the second, which appears in the npmrc:
> https://nexus3.onap.org/repository/npm.public/
> 
> Note: the first URL is needed to download the npm.tar.gz, while the second is needed to download all of its components.

I think the build picks up npmrc without any work on your part, so the only thing I think you need to do is change the URL in these yaml files to:
https://nexus3.onap.org/repository/npm.public/npm/-/

Patch Set 1:

also, if you're PUSHING files to npm, then you would probably need a third URL, but I have no experience with that
Patch Set 2: Published edit on patch set 1.
Patch Set 3: Published edit on patch set 2.

Patch Set 3: Verified+1
